entry.title: Fenix Simulations Updates A320 Lineup for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Fenix Simulations has recently issued an update to their A320 lineup for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, addressing issues with SU5 (2024) and the charts app, and bringing the Navdata cycle to the latest free cycle (2503). Update 2.4.0.4720 is primarily a hotfix, addressing incompatibilities with SU5 (2024 only), fixing the charts app so it displays a map for users without an active Navigraph subscription, and updating the Navdata cycle to the latest free one (2503). The complete changelog follows below: ‍2.4.0.4720 Avionics and Systems Art and Sound EFB The A320 Lineup is available in their store, starting from $67.62. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FlightSimExpo 2026 Registration Opens April 18, Microsoft in attendance
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. FlightSimExpo 2026 is just two months away, and the organisers have now provided an update on what attendees can expect this summer, including registration timelines and tours, early exhibitor confirmations, and on-site activities. Most notably, attendee registration for tours and experiences are set to open this weekend, two phases. FSA Captains get priority access from 12:00 CDT on April 18, while general attendees can begin booking 24 hours later. These add-on experiences are positioned as a core part of the weekend, and this year’s lineup leans heavily into both aviation access and social events. Attendees can book an airside bus tour of Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport, a guided visit to Delta’s Technical Operations facilities, and a range of other activities, including a 5K “fun run”, a brewery walking tour through downtown Saint Paul, and a group trip to the Mall of America. You can find more information on everything related to this at https://flightsimexpo.com/tours. In May, a second wave of sign-ups for exhibit hall activities will open. The planned activities in the exhibit hall include: You can find more information on the exhibit hall signups at flightsimexpo.com/signups. On the exhibitor side, during the recent developer livestream, Microsoft confirmed that the Microsoft Flight Simulator team will be present on the show floor. They will be part of a wider exhibitor lineup expected to exceed 60 participants, although the full list has not yet been released. This reveal is coming later this month, and according to the organisers, it has already been filmed on location in Saint Paul. FlightSimExpo 2026 takes place June 12–14 at the Saint Paul RiverCentre, located roughly 8 miles from MSP airport. The location offers over 130 direct routes to MSP and rail connections via Amtrak’s Borealis and Empire Builder services. If you have not yet booked a ticket, it would be wise to do this now, as tickets are selling out. The discounted $ 159-per-night hotel rate at the InterContinental is expected to sell out quickly, and prices may rise further depending on external factors, including NHL playoff activity in the same area. On-site, #FSExpoFriday will again host major product announcements before the exhibit hall opens for the evening, followed by full-day access across Saturday and Sunday. Alongside this, attendees can expect seminars, Q&A sessions, workshops, and panel discussions running throughout the weekend. There are also some new additions, premiering at this year’s event. One of them is the FlightSimExpo Combat Arena, which introduces a more interactive, competitive element, featuring air-combat-style challenges and team-based activities. Beyond aviation, the show floor will feature other simulation sectors, such as racing and rail. flightsimexpo.com/schedule The Media Partner Lounge is set to return, supported by several community creators, including Closed Traffic Podcast, FactualGull, Fly With Alyson, Mr Squirrel Lee, and Will Shaw. It will again serve as a central meeting point for media, creators, and attendees. There is also an option for partners to host meet-and-greet sessions and feature branding in the space, with a registration deadline of May 1. A full weekend pass costs $85, and single-day tickets range from $35 to $55 depending on the day. As always, FSA Captains receive a 20% discount and priority access to certain activities. For those not attending in person, an online access option will again be available, offering live and on-demand access to seminars, a virtual exhibit hall walkthrough, and community access via Discord. ‍ Threshold will be in attendance at the event, and we will keep you updated on everything that happens, leading up to and during FlightSimExpo 2026. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Microsoft Hosts April 2026 Developer Stream, incl. SU5, AAU4, WU21 and more
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Microsoft has published its April 2026 developer update, following a lengthy livestream. It was hosted on Twitch, now available on YouTube as well. Sim Update 5 is currently targeting release between Apr. 20 and Apr. 24, pending resolution of a small number of remaining issues. The update brings PSVR2 support on PlayStation 5, the first batch of third-party add-ons on the PS5 marketplace, and a long-awaited weather radar with tilt functionality. Alongside that, work on improving performance has continued, with further optimisation of multi-threading, memory allocation, and heavy aircraft scenarios. A highlight of this update is an updated airflow simulation. This introduces improved turbulence modelling, better wake interaction, and more realistic behaviour inside clouds, including density changes caused by thermals. Visualisation tools have been expanded, now using colour grading to indicate airflow strength. Aircraft and Avionics Update 4 will release alongside SU5, bringing several aircraft up to native MSFS 2024 standards. This includes the ATR 42/72, Antonov An-2, Fokker F.VII, Latécoère 631, and Savoia-Marchetti S.55, with further updates planned. These upgrades focus on improved system depth, visuals, and integration with newer sim features. Looking slightly ahead, the next World Update will focus on Australia, significantly expanding coverage here. Included will be over 30 new areas of interest, multiple handcrafted airports including Broome and Coober Pedy, and a set of new activities. Local Legend 24 will be releasing alongside this update, the CAC Boomerang, a WWII aircraft developed by iniBuilds. Two aircraft releases are planned. The Piper M600, developed by FSReborn, will be released as Expert Series 02 in June. Later in the year, the Embraer Praetor 600 will follow as Expert Series 03. Microsoft also said bug fixing remains the top focus, described internally as a “zero bug initiative”, even if that is not realistically achievable. Marketplace improvements are also planned, with curated content, gifting support, and a more controlled rollout on PlayStation, all in the pipeline. Microsoft will also be in attendance at FlightSimExpo 2026. Read more about that here. ‍ Watch the full developer stream on Twitch and YouTube. Read it in the written format here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Releases High Detail Update for 737-600, Post-Release Update for 737-700HDU
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has released its latest round of updates for the 737NG series, with the 737-600 now receiving the awaited High Detail Update, while the 737-700 has received a post-release patch with additional visual changes, system improvements, and some teething fixes. As headlined, the 737-600 is now part of the lineup with the High Detail Update, something PMDG first outlined back in November 2025 and later expanded in March. The update includes a rework of the aircraft model, with better geometry, improved textures and more. PMDG says that the changes go deep into the model, with tens of thousands of additional rivets, screws, and fasteners modelled, alongside updated surface mapping, improved animations on external panels and hatches, and reworked ground equipment. Further features are updates to the cockpit, cabin, cargo areas, and gear bays, built using the MSFS 2024 SDK. Alongside the 737-600 update, both the 737-600 and 737-700 have gotten a set of system and stability improvements. These include improved WASM stability, performance improvements in tablet map rendering, and the integration of BeyondATC datalink capability. As usual, the full list of changes is available through the PMDG Operations Center. A post-release update for the 737-700 has already been submitted to the MSFS Marketplace, also available directly through PMDG’s own store and the Operations Center. Updates for the 737-900 and 737-800 are planned next, in that order, though no exact timeline has been provided yet. Because of the scale of changes introduced with the High Detail Update, all official liveries distributed via the Operations Center have been updated. Third-party liveries created prior to HDU may require adjustments from their authors, and compatibility can vary depending on how they were built. ‍ The update is available now via the PMDG Operations Center, with a Marketplace release to follow once Microsoft completes its intake process. ‍ Full update post on PMDG’s forums. ‍ Separately, but worth noting, PMDG has also put out a call for real-world input, seeking individuals who have worked as crew on the Boeing 747-100 or other early 747 “classic” variants. Contact them here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Fly X Simulations Releases Belfast City Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Fly X Simulations has released its rendition of George Best Belfast City Airport (EGAC), a compact airport in Northern Ireland, for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024. The scenery is now available via the iniBuilds store. Located a few minutes out from central Belfast, EGAC is an important regional gateway, with the airlines operating there including Aer Lingus, British Airways, easyJet, and Loganair, serving routes all across the UK and to nearby European destinations. The release includes a full, high-detail airport model of the terminal and its interior, with custom PBR ground textures and accurate stand markings, and hand-placed clutter, including service vehicles and static cars. ‍ The package also comes with a custom GSX profile by default. ‍ Fly X Simulations’ Belfast City Airport is now available on the iniBuilds store, priced at $16.27 USD, for both MSFS 2020 and 2024. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: ‍Dreamflight Studios Provides Update on Teterboro V2, Guanacaste V2
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Dreamflight Studios has provided an update on two of its airport projects, starting with a first proper look at Teterboro V2, followed by a clarification on the status of Guanacaste V2, both of which appear to have grown well beyond their original scope. Starting with Teterboro, the developer has revealed that a full V2 rebuild is on the way, marking six years since the original release. Rather than iterating on the existing version, Dreamflight has rebuilt the airport entirely from the ground up, aiming to bring it in line with its current standards. It will include upgraded textures, reworked models, and a more cohesive overall build across the airport. Teterboro’s role as a major business aviation hub just outside New York City is also worth mentioning, with Dreamflight highlighting seven fully recreated FBOs, each with detailed interiors. We do not yet have a release date for the project, but it looks to be shaping up nicely. You can watch a preview stream of the scenery below: In a separate post, Dreamflight addressed MRLBv2, confirming that the project is still in active development after a period of silence. What was initially planned as a smaller update has since evolved into a full rebuild, after the team revisited the original assets and found they no longer met the quality level of their newer work. This shift explains Dreamflight’s silence, with the developer acknowledging the communication gap while noting that progress is now well underway. According to Dreamflight, MRLBv2 is “not far away” and will be released as a free update for existing users. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: SoFly Announces Cardiff Airport for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. SoFly has announced its first release in the X-Plane ecosystem, confirming that Cardiff Airport (EGFF) is in development for X-Plane 12. The project is being developed in collaboration with Sun Studios and is based on SoFly’s existing rendition of Cardiff for MSFS, now brought across and adapted for Laminar’s platform. The developer says the airport has been reworked to take advantage of XP12’s lighting and weather systems, with a focus on making the environment feel more reactive to conditions. SoFly highlights dynamic surfaces across the airport, including wet taxiways during rain, and snow accumulation affecting ground equipment and vehicles when conditions call for it. The scenery aims to reflect the current real-world layout, coming with updated textures and refined modelling throughout. The developer points to several smaller details around the field, including the British Airways maintenance hangar. Multiple stands will also feature SAM jetways. SoFly has not provided a confirmed price or exact release date, though both are expected to follow soon, alongside a full trailer. ‍ Read the full press release here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Sim Design Group Releases Giza Sphinx for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Sim Design Group has recently released Sphinx International Airport (HESX), located just outside Cairo and minutes from the Giza Pyramids, for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, bringing one of Egypt’s newest airports into the sim. As a gateway to one of the most recognisable landmarks in the world, Giza Sphinx offers a bit of a different flying experience compared to its busier siblings across Egypt, serving a lot of low-cost traffic, private aviation, and short-haul operations. The scenery includes a fully modelled terminal and surrounding airport buildings that match the real-world architecture, proportions, and materials. It is built on an accurate layout based on current data, covering runway geometry, taxiways, apron structure, and parking stands. Custom textures have been used across the airfield, with visible wear, surface variation, and various markings. Custom night lighting and a custom GSX profile is also included. Sphinx International Airport is now available for $16.28 USD, and is compatible with both MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: France VFR Releases Poitiers-Biard for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. France VFR has released Poitiers-Biard Airport (LFBI) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, continuing the ongoing Airports France series. Located just west of Poitiers, in the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region, LFBI is a compact international airport, with operators Air France, Ryanair and Volotea flying out of it. The scenery includes accurately modelled terminal buildings, surrounding structures, ground textures, and markings, alongside a mix of static and animated objects placed across the apron. The airport features custom night lighting and is built on the latest layouts, aligned with the current charts and reference data. Further included are high-resolution textures, photo-realistic surfaces across the airfield, and custom 3D buildings. Vegetation around the airport has also been worked on, helping soften the transition between the airfield and the surrounding MSFS scenery. The package comes with some additional material, including SIA, VAC, IAC, APT, and ARRDEP charts. ‍ LFBI Poitiers-Biard is available now for $11.65 USD, excluding taxes, with a download size of around 900 MB, and supports both MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Navigraph Publishes Community Survey 2026 Results
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Navigraph recently shared the results of their annual Community Survey, which gathered a record-breaking 42,000 responses and provided insights into the most popular platforms, add-ons, simulation habits, most commonly used hardware, and more. Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 now takes the leadership of the popularity chart with a whopping 49.5% market share, followed by Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 with 27.8%, totaling up to nearly 80% combined, meaning it has gained ground since 2023, when Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 (2024 didn’t exist yet) had 70.8% of the market. In relation to last year’s survey, 2024 has now overtaken 2020, but the numbers show that 2020 still has its loyalists. X-Plane 12, on the other hand, remains stagnant at 11%, while X-Plane 11 and P3D feature in the bottom row with less than 2% each. The survey also shows that most simmers who primarily used Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 migrated to 2024, as reflected in the new popularity chart. Former X-Plane 11 simmers went over to Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 instead of X-Plane 12, though, reflecting an issue with both platforms: VRAM demands are too high, so people with less powerful graphics cards are forced to keep using DirectX 11-based platforms. ​ As for the popular aircraft add-ons go, Fenix (42.4%), PMDG (31.5% and 23.3%), and iniBuilds (15.7%) lead the Microsoft Flight Simulator chart, whereas ToLiss (37.2%, 11.2%, and 9.2%), Zibo (29.8%), and Flight Factor (17.1%) lead the X-Plane chart. Click here to dive into the full details of Navigraph’s latest survey. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their TriStar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a wide range of fixes across core systems to improve stability and day-to-day usability. Update v1.0.3 includes key fixes to autopilot/AFCS behavior, INS navigation logic, fuel and electrical systems, engine operation, and various cockpit interactions and bindings, alongside tweaks to flight handling, including smoother pitch trim, refined autothrottle behavior, and more accurate climb performance. Furthermore, additional fixes have been applied across visuals, PFB usability, and sounds (corrected callouts and improved audio consistency). The complete changelog follows below: V1.0.3SYSTEMS ⚙️ ART 🎨 PFB📜 SOUNDS🔊 The add-on is available in the MSFS Marketplace for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: SoFly’s Cardiff Airport for X-Plane 12 Releasing Apr. 23
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. SoFly has released a new trailer for its upcoming Cardiff Airport (EGFF) scenery for X-Plane 12, marking its first dedicated release for the platform. The scenery is now confirmed to release on Thursday, Apr. 23. SoFly head Calum shared more context in a Reddit post, explaining that the team has spent most of its time on other simulators recently, but now wants to bring more UK airports into X-Plane. Cardiff was chosen because that area “needed some TLC” and offers quite a bit of character for its relatively small size. SoFly followed up with a blog post confirming that Cardiff Airport for XP12 will release on April 23, 2026, and will be available exclusively through the iniBuilds store at launch, with other storefronts planned later. Cardiff has a scenic coastal approach and also has a slightly unusual operational role. In addition to regular scheduled flights from Ryanair and TUI, the airport is also used by British Airways as a maintenance base for parts of its widebody fleet. The airport is recreated in great detail and will be brought up to a modern XP12 standard. As SoFly’s first XP12 project, the team is seeking community feedback, with them suggesting that more X-Plane content could follow if the demand is there. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightSimWeekend 2027 to Be Held in Utrecht Apr. 3–4
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. After years at the Aviodrome museum in Lelystad, Flight Sim Weekend has today announced that Utrecht, The Netherlands, will be the new location for its 2027 edition. The organisers have confirmed that FlightSimWeekend 2027 will take place on Apr. 3-4 at Expo Houten in Utrecht, a dedicated exhibition venue just outside the city. According to FSWeekend, the move comes after a period of major growth, with attendance at the 2026 edition climbing to nearly 5,000 visitors, a record for the show, and enough to justify moving to a larger, more professional exhibition space. Utrecht is located centrally in the Netherlands, and the new venue offers significantly easier access for both domestic and international visitors. Utrecht Centraal, the main railway station, is nearby, and Amsterdam Schiphol Airport is roughly 45 minutes away, making travel more straightforward for exhibitors and attendees alike. The Netherlands' national train operator, NS, has direct train connections from Schipol Airport to Utrecht several times every hour, with Utrecht being a major rail junction. The move marks a significant turning point, as Lelystad and the Aviodrome have been closely tied to FlightSimWeekend for years, with the location serving as an aviation museum. Utrecht offers a, perhaps, more conventional trade-show setting, but the FSWeekend team notes a significant capacity boost, expanded floor space, improved layouts, and better connectivity and comfort. According to the venue, Expo Houten offers 10,000 square metres of exhibition space across four ground-floor halls, a congress area for around 350 guests, a wider event space, alongside around 10 meeting spaces. The site is directly off the A27 motorway, including 1,650 free parking spaces, and can also be reached by bus from Utrecht Centraal or Houten station. Read the full press release here. Visit the FlightSimWeekend website here. ‍ More details on 2027 exhibitors, schedules, and tickets are expected to follow in the coming weeks. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A340 Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their A340 Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing stability and performance improvements in the WASM environment, along with core aircraft systems fixes. Update v1.0.11 introduces under-the-hood improvements to memory management, data efficiency, and system robustness, improving overall consistency during high-load periods in the simulator. Furthermore, the update also addresses system-level issues affecting day-to-day operations, including corrections to flight deck indications, trim and hydraulic logic, navigation behavior, and MCDU interactions. They have greatly improved the 'add-ons' performance and WASM stability by optimizing memory handling, reducing unnecessary data copying in key systems, lowering transient memory usage, and improving overall efficiency. ​ On top of that, they have refined data access patterns and added additional safety checks across critical memory pathways to guard against invalid states and edge cases. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ V1.0.11 SYSTEMS ⚙️ FIXED Deviation indication when no course tuned in VOR FIXED Trim can be moved without hydraulic pressure FIXED Issues with clearing scratchpad FIXED Fix page formatting issues FIXED Manually tuned navaids not resetting after landing FIXED THS should be +4 deviation on cold and dark load ‍ The add-on is available on the iniBuilds Store for roughly $60.84, requiring at least 7.02 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A350 Airliner for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their A350 Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020/2024, bringing stability and performance improvements in the WASM environment, along with core aircraft systems fixes. ​ Update v1.2.5 introduces under-the-hood improvements to memory management, data efficiency, and system robustness, improving overall consistency during high-load periods in the simulator. ​ Furthermore, the update includes a comprehensive set of fixes across key aircraft systems, including FMGS logic, flight guidance behavior, navigation predictions, and MCDU functionality. Additionally, they also updated the EFB, sounds, and the aircraft’s model. They have greatly improved the 'add-ons' performance and WASM stability by optimizing memory handling, reducing unnecessary data copying in key systems, lowering transient memory usage, and improving overall efficiency. ​ On top of that, they have refined data access patterns and added additional safety checks across critical memory pathways to guard against invalid states and edge cases. The complete changelog follows below: V1.2.5SYSTEMS ⚙️FIXED Position time manual behaviourFIXED Direct to behaviourFIXED Fix page issueFIXED Issue with Top of climb predictionFIXED TO page not locking in fields when TO phase activeFIXED Fuel & load pageFIXED The aircraft GW & GWCG should be calculated/shown on the ED/PERMANENT DATAFIXED ALT CSTR on NDFIXED Issues with making waypoint-bearingFIXED Deleting pilot waypoints selection exceeds the remaining number if waypointsFIXED T/C marker missing in OP CLBFIXED Altitude selection & flight phase logic during cruiseFIXED Green dot speed mismatchFIXED "NOISE END" box appears on top of the previous "SPD CSTR"FIXED FD Behaviour - Vertical Guidance Timing & Wind Shift Sensitivity ART 🎨FIXED some bolts & other small parts are missing from the landing gear, & some are not attached to anythingFIXED APPR button not animatedFIXED EVA command guard does not openFIXED Ext Model - Sun shades are mounted on exterior window instead of the interior onesFIXED Rudder trim animation inverted EFB📱FIXED Enroute map token loss in flightFIXED Enroute map 403 error due to bad API call SOUNDS🔊FIXED Missing "retard" calloutFIXED No windshear alert ‍ The add-on is available on the iniBuilds Store for roughly $81.13, requiring at least 13.56 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Updates Frankfurt Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has recently issued a major update to their rendition of Frankfurt Airport (EDDF) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, bringing the ground layout around Terminal 3 up to current standards, adding the new taxiway “s”, and many other fixes. Update 1.1.2 brings the ground layout around Terminal 3 to its current state as of April 2026, adds the new taxiway “s”, adds and corrects multiple taxisigns, fixes the terraforming at the intersection between “N14” and “N”, fixes spelling on markings at the de-icing pad 2, added animated jetways to terminal 3, changes the Aerosoft VDGS models to the new “Safedock X” system, adjusts jetway placement to prevent clipping when docked to an airplane, and adjusts the minimum and maximum rotation values on jetways A50-A69. ​ It’s available on the Aerosoft Shop for roughly $24.99, requiring at least 3.7 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍
